 {"id":"bd-5iz","title":"ffs-block: Implement ARC metadata cache (read-only first)","description":"Goal: implement an ARC-like cache wrapper for block reads to reduce random I/O during metadata-heavy operations.

Scope (v1):
- Cache read_block() results (BlockNumber -> BlockBuf).
- ARC lists: T1/T2 + ghost lists B1/B2; adaptive parameter p.
- No write-back yet; writes either bypass or invalidate.

Key design questions to answer explicitly:
- Cache key: BlockNumber only, or (BlockNumber, CommitSeq/Version) for MVCC? (If MVCC needs versioned cache, design now.)
- Concurrency: avoid holding cache locks while doing disk I/O.

Acceptance:
- Unit tests cover core ARC transitions (hits/misses/evictions) with deterministic sequences.
- No deadlocks; no I/O under locks.
- Benchmarks show cache improves repeated metadata reads on fixture images.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:15:43.959932112Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T17:07:09.719979271Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T17:07:09.719959934Z","close_reason":"ARC cache: fix eviction logic + add transition tests; gates green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["io"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5iz","depends_on_id":"bd-14w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:16:27.296806735Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-5s0r","title":"EPIC: Write-Back Cache","description":"# EPIC: Write-Back Cache

## PURPOSE
Implement a write-back cache layer between FUSE write operations and disk I/O. This is critical for write performance — without write-back caching, every FUSE write would require immediate disk I/O (write-through), making FrankenFS orders of magnitude slower than kernel filesystems.

## DESIGN

### Phased Approach
1. DirtyTracker: track which cached blocks have been modified but not flushed
2. FlushDaemon: background thread that periodically flushes dirty blocks to disk
3. MVCC Integration: dirty block tracking integrates with MVCC version chains

### Core Invariant
**Dirty blocks must never be evicted from cache until flushed to disk.**
Violating this invariant causes data loss (dirty data discarded without writing).

### DirtyTracker
- Per-block dirty flag in cache metadata
- Dirty list: ordered by age (oldest dirty block first)
- Statistics: dirty block count, dirty data size, oldest dirty age

### FlushDaemon
- Background thread wakes periodically (configurable: default 5s)
- Flushes oldest dirty blocks first (LRU-like)
- Flush batch size: configurable (default 256 blocks)
- Integrates with fsync: explicit fsync flushes all dirty blocks for that inode
- Backpressure: if dirty ratio exceeds threshold (e.g., 80%), block new writes until flushed

### MVCC Integration
- Dirty blocks belong to a specific MVCC version
- Flush writes the committed version (not in-flight versions)
- Aborted transaction: dirty blocks discarded without flush
- Committed transaction: dirty blocks eligible for flush

### Eviction Coordination
- Cache eviction (ARC or S3-FIFO) must check dirty flag before evicting
- If dirty: flush first, then evict
- If flush fails: do NOT evict (keep dirty block in cache, retry later)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Dirty blocks tracked correctly (set on write, cleared on flush)
- [ ] FlushDaemon runs in background, flushes oldest dirty blocks
- [ ] fsync flushes all dirty blocks for specified inode
- [ ] Dirty blocks never evicted without flush (invariant enforced)
- [ ] Backpressure prevents OOM from too many dirty blocks
- [ ] MVCC integration: only committed versions flushed
- [ ] Benchmark: write throughput with write-back vs write-through

## Success Criteria
1. All children closed
2. E2E durability test (bd-5s0r.4) passes
3. Dirty blocks never evicted without flush (invariant enforced)
4. Write throughput >= 80pct of kernel ext4 write-back
5. fsync flushes all dirty blocks for target inode","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:26:02.706174558Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T23:46:05.116910898Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T23:46:05.116892123Z","close_reason":"All children closed. Write-Back Cache epic is done.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cache","durability","writeback"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5s0r","depends_on_id":"bd-huh","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:31:38.262086688Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":93,"issue_id":"bd-5s0r","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SECOND-PASS REVIEW (lint fix): Epic is missing ## Success Criteria section. Add measurable success criteria before any children are marked closed. Example format: '## Success Criteria\n1. All children closed\n2. E2E test suite passes\n3. Feature parity gate green'","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:05:41Z"},{"id":132,"issue_id":"bd-5s0r","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Cluster: concurrency_perf
Mapped graveyard sections: 14.10 EBR + 15.1 S3-FIFO + 15.4 Parallel WAL
EV score: 13.333333333333334 (Impact*Confidence*Reuse / (Effort*Friction))
Priority tier: S/A
Budgeted mode: max_chain_len=64, wal_flush_batch=256, dirty_ratio_cap=0.8
Fallback trigger: single-writer conservative mode with bounded queue

Required proof artifacts for execution on this bead:
- Baseline + hotspot evidence before changes.
- Isomorphism proof block per lever.
- Evidence-ledger event for decision path + fallback invocation.
- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:20Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-5s0r.1","title":"Implement DirtyTracker for write-back cache","description":"# Implement DirtyTracker for write-back cache

## GOAL
Track which cached blocks have been modified but not yet flushed to disk. This is the foundation of the write-back cache.

## DESIGN
- Per-block dirty flag in cache entry metadata
- DirtyList: ordered collection of dirty block IDs, sorted by modification time
- On block write: set dirty flag, add to DirtyList with current timestamp
- On flush: clear dirty flag, remove from DirtyList
- Statistics: dirty_count, dirty_bytes, oldest_dirty_age, dirty_ratio

### Integration with Cache
- Modify BlockCache trait to include dirty tracking
- Cache::write(block_id, data) -> sets dirty
- Cache::mark_clean(block_id) -> clears dirty (called after flush)
- Cache::dirty_blocks() -> iterator over dirty blocks (oldest first)
- Cache::evict(block_id) -> panics if dirty (invariant enforcement)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Dirty flag correctly set on write, cleared on flush
- [ ] DirtyList maintains age ordering
- [ ] Cache::evict panics if block is dirty (invariant)
- [ ] Statistics accurately reflect dirty state
- [ ] Unit tests: write/flush/evict lifecycle","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:26:11.920623498Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:56:12.221669971Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T18:56:12.221650535Z","close_reason":"Implemented DirtyTracker ordering/stats, strict dirty-eviction invariant, structured logging, and unit lifecycle tests in ffs-block.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cache","dirty","tracker"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5s0r.1","depends_on_id":"bd-5s0r","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:26:11.920623498Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":75,"issue_id":"bd-5s0r.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SECOND-PASS REVIEW: This bead is missing a ## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS section. Every implementation bead must specify structured log events at trace/debug/info/warn/error levels with field names. Add logging spec before implementation begins.","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:04:17Z"},{"id":101,"issue_id":"bd-5s0r.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"LOGGING REQUIREMENTS implemented in crates/ffs-block/src/lib.rs for this bead: trace events , , ; debug event  and ; info events  and ; warn event ; error events  and . All include structured fields (block, bytes, dirty_blocks, dirty_bytes, dirty_ratio, oldest_dirty_age_ticks, flush counters) to satisfy auditability.","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:55:49Z"},{"id":102,"issue_id":"bd-5s0r.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"LOGGING REQUIREMENTS implemented in `crates/ffs-block/src/lib.rs` for this bead.

Structured events added:
- trace: cache_write, mark_clean, cache_evict_clean
- debug: pending_flush_batch_start, flush_dirty_start
- info: pending_flush_batch_complete, flush_dirty_complete
- warn: dirty_evict_attempt
- error: pending_flush_batch_failed, flush_dirty_failed

Each event emits operational fields including block, bytes, dirty_blocks, dirty_bytes, dirty_ratio, oldest_dirty_age_ticks, and flush counters where applicable.
","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:55:54Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-5s0r.2","title":"Implement FlushDaemon for periodic dirty block writeback","description":"# Implement FlushDaemon for periodic dirty block writeback

## GOAL
Background daemon that periodically flushes dirty blocks from cache to disk, providing write-back semantics without requiring explicit fsync for every write.

## DESIGN
- Background thread spawned on mount, stopped on unmount
- Wake interval: configurable (default 5 seconds)
- Each wake cycle: iterate DirtyList, flush oldest blocks first
- Batch size: configurable (default 256 blocks per cycle)
- fsync integration: explicit fsync bypasses daemon, flushes immediately

### Backpressure
- dirty_ratio = dirty_bytes / cache_capacity
- If dirty_ratio > high_watermark (80%): flush aggressively (all dirty blocks)
- If dirty_ratio > critical_watermark (95%): block new writes until flushed below high
- Prevents OOM from unbounded dirty accumulation

### Shutdown
- On clean unmount: flush ALL dirty blocks before releasing FUSE
- On signal (SIGTERM): flush all dirty blocks, then exit
- On SIGKILL: dirty blocks lost (handled by crash recovery)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] FlushDaemon runs in background, flushes periodically
- [ ] Oldest dirty blocks flushed first
- [ ] Backpressure blocks writes when dirty ratio critical
- [ ] Clean unmount flushes all dirty blocks
- [ ] Unit tests: daemon lifecycle, flush ordering
- [ ] Integration test: write 1000 blocks, verify all flushed within 2 intervals","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:26:22.891642122Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T19:05:17.763972094Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T19:05:17.763945294Z","close_reason":"Implemented FlushDaemon lifecycle, batch oldest-first flushing, high/critical dirty-ratio backpressure, shutdown drain, and daemon/backpressure tests in ffs-block.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cache","daemon","flush"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5s0r.2","depends_on_id":"bd-5s0r","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:26:22.891642122Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-5s0r.2","depends_on_id":"bd-5s0r.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:31:27.126296516Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":76,"issue_id":"bd-5s0r.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SECOND-PASS REVIEW: This bead is missing a ## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS section. Every implementation bead must specify structured log events at trace/debug/info/warn/error levels with field names. Add logging spec before implementation begins.","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:04:17Z"},{"id":103,"issue_id":"bd-5s0r.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS

Planned structured logging for `bd-5s0r.2` implementation:
- trace: `flush_daemon_tick` (cycle_seq, dirty_blocks, dirty_bytes, dirty_ratio), `flush_daemon_sleep` (interval_ms)
- debug: `flush_batch_start` (batch_len, oldest_block, oldest_dirty_age_ticks, policy)
- info: `flush_batch_complete` (flushed_blocks, flushed_bytes, duration_ms, remaining_dirty_blocks, remaining_dirty_ratio)
- warn: `flush_backpressure_high` (dirty_ratio, high_watermark), `flush_backpressure_critical` (dirty_ratio, critical_watermark)
- error: `flush_batch_failed` (error, attempted_blocks, attempted_bytes), `flush_shutdown_failed` (error, remaining_dirty_blocks)

All events will include stable field names and be emitted from the daemon lifecycle, backpressure path, and shutdown drain path.
","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:56:42Z"}]}
